# UC-18.1.3 · Endpoint Mobility Tracking — fvCEp Moves, Leaf Switches, and Host-Mobility Storm Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Inventory, Security &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep a simple ledger of where each device’s network name tag last sat on your row of switches—like noting which park bench a courier used—so when it zips bench to bench faster than any real delivery, we notice before someone else’s name is on the package.*

---

## Description

Splunk correlates Cisco Application Centric Infrastructure endpoint Managed Objects—**fvCEp** rows exposing lifecycle counters (**lcC**, **lcOwn**), encapsulation (**encap**), DN lineage (**dn**) tied to tenants or endpoint groups, child objects **fvIp** for IPv4/IPv6 bindings, **fvRsHyper** hypervisor stitches, **fvRsCEpToPathEp** resolving leaf/port (**fabricNode**, **ifId**) attachments—with syslog semantics **F0856 EP-MOVE-NOTIFY**, **F0855**, and VLAN-centric **F0467** narratives distinct from UC-wide fault KPIs. Dataplane glean supplements controller commits: switches learn MAC/IP via ARP/GARP while APIC synchronizes COOP so Splunk contrasts planned relocation versus aberrant oscillations—split-brain failover bursts, MAC mobility loops across paired leaf IDs, stretched **fvCtx** moves forcing anycast-gateway SVI re-homing, or CNI churn punching underlay mappings. Field fidelity (**learnSrc**, **vrfDn**, **encap**) separates glean-driven attaches from programmatic pushes when telemetry diverges from Endpoint Tracker summaries.

## Value

Healthy workloads relocate deliberately; hostile or broken ones hop leaf switches faster than orchestration intends—spoofed MAC/IP pairs or churn storms fracture east-west trust assumptions before audits notice custody drift. Splunk mobility telemetry preserves operational fingerprints so migrations backed by CMDB change tickets stay visibly distinct from rogue NIC personalities bouncing across spine-facing attachments.

## Implementation

**Quick win:** Schedule **`aci_uc1813_endpoint_mobility_storm`** hourly over **`earliest=-4h@m`** with **`head 50`** mailed to NetOps—surfaces **`mac_u`** rows breaching **`moves_per_min`** without dashboards. Deploy **`Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`** syslog alongside scripted **`fvCEp`**/**`fvIp`** class collectors into **`index=cisco_aci`**; normalize **`mac`**, **`leaf_id`**, **`vrfDn`** via **`props.conf`** aliases; attach **`planned_motion_tag`** lookups fed by ServiceNow CHG exports.

## Evidence

Saved search **`aci_uc1813_endpoint_mobility_storm`** (index **`cisco_aci`**, **`cisco:aci:endpoint`** + **`cisco:aci:syslog`**) retained 400 days plus Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_aci_endpoint_mobility.json`** with drill tokens **`tok_mac`**/**`tok_epg`**.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- APIC clusters on supported 6.0(x) / 6.1(x) releases with HTTPS **443/tcp** reachable from Splunk collectors; dedicate read-only automation credentials separate from interactive administrators.
- Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**, 2.0.x field releases) wherever APIC and fabric syslog terminates so **cisco:aci:syslog** ingests mnemonic strings pertinent to endpoint mobility (**F0856 EP-MOVE-NOTIFY**, **F0855**, **F0467**) as peer evidence to REST-fed **fvCEp** snapshots.
- Layer **rest_ta** (Splunkbase **1546**) or Python modular inputs that POST **aaaLogin.json**, refresh via **aaaRefresh.json** (~10 minute cookie/token TTL), then GET **/api/node/class/fvCEp.json**, **/api/node/class/fvIp.json**, **/api/node/class/fvCEpToPathEp.json**—tag streams **cisco:aci:endpoint** for structured pulls plus optional **cisco:aci:fault**/**cisco:aci:audit** when faults duplicate aaaModLR edits.
- RBAC remains sensitive because automation accounts traverse tenant DN scopes—segment collectors when auditors demand separation between statistics pulls versus scripted reconciliation touching fvCEp subtrees.
- Capacity planning: polling dense fvCEp trees across multiple pods explodes cardinality—budget indexer EPS when joins multiply **dn_lc** × **mo_class** facets ahead of alerting.

### Step 1 — Data sources & ingest paths
(1) Syslog lane: validate **cisco:aci:syslog** surfaces EP-move narratives—not merely optics faults—before trusting mobility ratios derived solely from REST deltas lagging syslog by minutes.
(2) REST lane: schedule **60–120s** pulls for fvCEp plus children **fvIp**, **fvRsHyper**, **fvRsCEpToPathEp** exposing **fabricNode**, **ifId**, **encap**, **lcC**, **lcOwn**, **learnSrc**—flatten APIC attributes consistently across collectors.
(3) Binding lane: ingest fvRsCEpToPathEp rows tying MAC/IP placements to leaf/path MO references so dashboards reconcile leaf-span anomalies without guessing from syslog fragments alone.
(4) Hypervisor lane: capture fvRsHyper attachments whenever VMware overlays correlate underlay moves—helps differentiate **vMotion** bursts from silent spoof loops riding duplicate MAC addresses.
(5) Telemetry lane: optional NX-OS dial-out complements syslog when EP-move bursts arrive faster than REST polls—still correlate against fvCEp snapshots before paging security bridges.

### Step 2 — Field extractions, props/transforms
Normalize naming drift via FIELDALIAS macros after running **fieldsummary** against raw JSON—critical fields include **dn**, **mac**, **ip**, **encap**, **lcC**, **lcOwn**, **name**, **vrfDn**, **ifId**, learnSrc, **epg**, **leaf_id**, **port_id**, fabricNode.
- **props.conf**: alias **mac**/**macAddr**→canonical **mac_u**; alias IPv4/IPv6 **fvIp.addr** strings before CIM overlays.
- **CIM Network_Traffic**: map mac_u→**src_mac**, **ip**→**dest_ip**, derive **app**/tenant hints via lookups on **fvAEPg** DN fragments—not raw syslog tokens alone.
- **CIM Inventory**: retain leaf_id, **port_id**, fabricNode, **dn** lineage for rack/asset reconciliation CSVs keyed off **aci_leaf_rack_inventory**.
- **transforms.conf**: **REPORT-aci_leaf_dn** extracts **pod**, **node**, **port** facets powering downstream **fabric_fanout** math inside SPL macros.

### Step 3 — Initial validation
(1) Compare Splunk mac_u timelines against APIC Operational Endpoint Tracker—timestamp drift hints duplicated ingestion transports or overlapping modular inputs authenticated to the same APIC VIP.
(2) Verify fvRsCEpToPathEp JSON retains **tDn** pointers expected before trusting flattened leaf_id columns—camelCase mismatches remediate via FIELDALIAS refreshes post TA upgrades.
(3) Harmonize timezone offsets—APIC REST timestamps serialize UTC whereas syslog might localize; enforce TZ=UTC on inputs or decorate dashboards with explicit offsets during DST transitions.
(4) Cross-check learnSrc spikes against CLI **show endpoint** counters from sampled fabricNode IDs anchoring investigative notebooks—not SPL thresholds alone on day one.

### Step 4 — SPL building blocks
Paste into a saved search once indexes align; extend **coalesce()** chains after running **fieldsummary** against raw JSON.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog") earliest=-4h@m
| eval mac_u=upper(trim(coalesce(mac, macAddr, endpoint_mac, "")))
| eval leaf_id=trim(coalesce(leaf_id, fabricNode, leaf_dn_id, ""))
| eval vrfDn=lower(trim(coalesce(vrfDn, vrf_dn, ctx_dn, "")))
| eval sys_move=if(match(_raw,"(?i)F0856|EP[-_ ]MOVE"),1,0)
| eval api_move=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:endpoint" AND match(lower(_raw),"move"),1,0)
| eval mobility_evt=max(sys_move, api_move)
| where mobility_evt>0 OR sourcetype=="cisco:aci:endpoint"
| sort mac_u _time
| streamstats window=120 global=f current=f latest(leaf_id) as prev_leaf by mac_u
| eval leaf_chg=if(isnotnull(prev_leaf) AND prev_leaf!=leaf_id AND len(leaf_id)>0,1,0)
| bin _time span=60s as minute_bin
| stats sum(leaf_chg) as moves_per_min dc(leaf_id) as leaf_fanout by mac_u minute_bin
| where moves_per_min>3 OR leaf_fanout>=4
```

Correlation notes
- **sort**/**streamstats** sequences preserve **prev_leaf** fidelity—misordered sorts destroy mobility narratives silently.
- **bin**/**stats** collapses pulses into **moves_per_min** buckets analogous to EP-move storms (**>3** moves inside aligned bins).
- **dc(leaf_id)** exposes leaf-span anomalies helpful when MAC mobility loops bounce between paired leaf IDs without exceeding simplistic rate counters alone.

### Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns (deepest section)
- CMDB lookups enrich mac_u with owning teams—pager annotations escalate PCI workloads differently than DEV sandboxes based on workload_segment metadata beside **planned_motion_tag** extracts refreshed nightly from Configuration Items.
- **ServiceNow CHG** CSV nightly dumps align mobility bursts with approved virtualization evacuations—macros mute paging when **CHG_APPROVED** overlaps offending bins while preserving forensic copies for auditors sampling vaulted archives.
- **Nexus Dashboard Insights** REST (**HTTPS /443**, OAuth secrets rotated quarterly) cross-links fabric anomalies with mobility KPI tiles—tie recommendation identifiers back into Splunk **notes** fields so responders pivot without juggling independent portals mid-incident (API namespaces vary per Cisco release—pin swagger references internally).
- **MITRE T1557.002** LLMNR/NBT-NS poisoning belongs only when gratuitous ARP storms coincide lacking sanctioned fvRsHyper migrations—avoid tagging benign VMware churn solely because syslog verbosity spikes during datastore maintenance unrelated to fvCEp MO edits.
- **COOP anchoring**: optional hourly **coopEp** snapshots summarized via collect searches anchor datastore deltas proving bursts preceded APIC commits versus dataplane glean storms flipping identities twice per minute across unrelated fabricNode IDs—still optional when collectors absent.
- **Kubernetes/CNI** joins enrich **fvAEPg** naming collisions whenever pod annotations expose tenant tags aligning overlay churn with underlay mobility timelines—differentiates CNIs restarting pods versus hostile MAC cloning attempts leveraging omitted hypervisor stitches.

### Step 6 — Operational runbook & troubleshooting modes
(1) Dashboard stack: single-value mobility storm counts, **timechart** **moves_per_min** by mac_u, heatmaps for **leaf_fanout**, drill tables tying leaf_id DNs to CMDB owners via **aci_leaf_rack_inventory**.
(2) Alerts on sustained storm velocity require CHG correlation—suppress macros honoring **planned_motion_tag** when VMware maintenance tickets reconcile bursts.
(3) Publish COOP flush advisory tiles during controller upgrades so teams connect syslog EP-move spikes with benign convergence—not hostile lateral movement hunts alone.
(4) Escalate Tier-2 **aci** SMEs when **distinct_leaf_hour** analogues exceed governance thresholds across consecutive bins without approvals recorded in CMDB extracts.
(5) Rotate automation secrets quarterly—failed **aaaRefresh** attempts surface duplicated REST rows with identical counters yet drifting **_indextime** offsets mimicking fabricated storms.
- MTTR target — median acknowledgement within **35 minutes** because lookups collapse MAC-to-team routing versus manual APIC hunts that averaged **48 minutes** across FY24 bridge reviews.
- MTBF expectation — tune **mobility_storm** thresholds until actionable escalations occur less than once every **720 hours** on steady-state fabrics excluding VMware-heavy maintenance quarters.


Troubleshooting modes:

- **Duplicate modular inputs** — multiple collectors authenticated to the same APIC VIP double-count endpoint pulls until enriching with collector_id metadata dedupe keys.

- **Null leaf_id columns** — DN flattening drift strips fabricNode integers—inspect **_raw** JSON and broaden **coalesce()** chains cautiously after vendor confirmation.

- **Syslog mobility without REST deltas** — hardware narratives may precede JSON aggregation—tie syslog **_time** windows to REST **_time** slices before paging NetSec SMEs.

- **vPC orphan quirks** — fvRsCEpToPathEp repoints across MCT members even when VMs remain logically stationary—inflate **leaf_fanout** benignly until paired VPC diagnostics reconcile attachments.

- **HTTP 429 storms** — widen intervals or shard MO pulls whenever APIC CPU saturates during massive tenant onboarding batches harvesting fvCEp subtrees repeatedly.

- **Collector TLS failures masked as sparse data** — splunkd logs reveal handshake errors faster than sparse dashboards hint—grep Heavy Forwarder logs before rewriting SPL.

Further enrichment anchors (still Step 5 depth): stitch Splunk mobility searches with nightly VMware **vCenter** extracts keyed by VM MAC addresses when overlays justify correlating fvRsHyper bindings against orchestrator-driven datastore evacuations documented outside APIC entirely—guards against declaring spoof storms whenever Splunk lacks virtualization context yet APIC endpoint DN scopes remain authoritative for attach/detach sequencing referenced inside Endpoint Tracker summaries.

Indexer sizing rehearsal: execute pilot buckets (`bucket _time span=1m | stats count`) against index=cisco_aci during mobility POC windows before enlarging REST poll parallelism beyond fvCEp-only baselines—capacity planners cite EPS envelopes whenever dc(leaf_id) cardinality spikes intersect duplicated modular inputs referencing identical aaaRefresh cookies.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-18.1.3 endpoint mobility storm macro placeholders — swap index macro + tenant filters before production.")`
| search index=cisco_aci
| search (`sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint"` OR `sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog"`) earliest=-4h@m
| eval mac_u=upper(trim(coalesce(mac, macAddr, endpoint_mac, "")))
| eval ip=trim(coalesce(ip, endpoint_ip, addr, ""))
| eval leaf_id=trim(coalesce(leaf_id, fabricNode, leaf_dn_id, ""))
| eval port_id=trim(coalesce(port_id, ifId, port_dn, ""))
| eval vrfDn=lower(trim(coalesce(vrfDn, vrf_dn, ctx_dn, "")))
| eval fabricNode=trim(coalesce(fabricNode, node_id, ""))
| eval learnSrc=trim(coalesce(learnSrc, learn_src, glean_src, ""))
| eval encap=trim(coalesce(encap, encap_label, ""))
| eval lc_c=lower(trim(coalesce(lcC, lc_c, "")))
| eval sys_move=if(match(_raw,"(?i)F0856|EP[-_ ]MOVE|EP[-_]MOBILITY"),1,0)
| eval api_move=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:endpoint" AND (match(lc_c,"(?i)modif") OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)move")),1,0)
| eval mobility_evt=max(sys_move, api_move)
| where mobility_evt>0 OR sourcetype=="cisco:aci:endpoint"
| fields _time mac_u ip leaf_id port_id vrfDn fabricNode learnSrc encap sourcetype host
| sort mac_u _time
| streamstats window=120 global=f current=f latest(leaf_id) as prev_leaf latest(_time) as prev_ts by mac_u
| eval leaf_chg=if(isnotnull(prev_leaf) AND prev_leaf!=leaf_id AND len(leaf_id)>0,1,0)
| bin _time span=60s as minute_bin
| stats sum(leaf_chg) as moves_per_min dc(leaf_id) as leaf_fanout dc(fabricNode) as fabric_fanout values(vrfDn) as vrfDn_vals values(learnSrc) as learn_vals max(encap) as encap_recent by mac_u ip minute_bin
| eval mobility_storm=if(moves_per_min>3 OR leaf_fanout>=4 OR fabric_fanout>=4,1,0)
| eval distinct_leaf_hour=leaf_fanout
| where mobility_storm=1 OR moves_per_min>=4 OR leaf_fanout>=5
| lookup aci_leaf_rack_inventory leaf_id OUTPUT rack_row cage_uid
| lookup endpoint_owner_registry mac AS mac_u OUTPUT owning_team workload_segment planned_motion_tag
| eval suppression_hint=if(planned_motion_tag="CHG_APPROVED","suppress_candidate","review")
| eval leaf_entropy_risk=if(distinct_leaf_hour>=6 AND suppression_hint="review","investigate_leaf_entropy","baseline_motion")
| sort - moves_per_min
| head 200
| table _time mac_u ip moves_per_min leaf_fanout vrfDn_vals learn_vals rack_row owning_team suppression_hint leaf_entropy_risk encap_recent
| `comment("Threshold recap: mobility storm >3 leaf_chg sums inside one-minute bins OR >=4 distinct leaf_ids fabric_fanout anomalies")`
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio **`ds_aci_endpoint_mobility`** — Row A **`splunk.singlevalue`** counting **`mobility_storm`** positives last hour plus **`splunk.timeline`** of **`moves_per_min`** split by **`mac_u`** top ten; Row B **`splunk.table`** leaf churn with **`rack_row`** lookup columns and **`suppression_hint`**; Row C **`splunk.column`** **`distinct_leaf_hour`** anomaly counts per **`workload_segment`**; Row D **`splunk.choropleth`** heat substituting **`rack_uid`** facets when **`aci_rack_geo.csv`** exists; Row E **`splunk.events`** drill tied to **`tok_mac`** token feeding underlying syslog **`F0856`** excerpts.

## Known False Positives

**VMware vMotion/DRS evacuation bursts** — coordinated **`fvCEp`** relocations spanning dozens of **`leaf_id`** entries mirror spoof storms yet align with **`CHG`** references tagging datastore maintenance—suppress **`mobility_storm`** macros whenever **`planned_motion_tag`** ties **`mac_u`** clusters to **`CHG004412`** style VMware windows while **`F0856`** narratives cite benign DPVM relocations distinct from **`F0467`** VLAN collisions (still noisy yet path-consistent). **vPC peer link renegotiation or member flaps** — **`fvRsCEpToPathEp`** may repoint through alternate MCT members even when workloads stay logically fixed, inflating **`leaf_fanout`**—require paired diagnostics from **`vpcInst`** syslog lines or STP guard logs before escalating; map suppressions to **`aci_vpc_suppress.csv`** keyed on **`vpcPairId`**. **Fabric image / F-Series line-card rolling upgrades** — COOP convergence and cache warm events duplicate endpoint learn events without attack significance; align splunk timelines with **`show version`** change tickets and pause **`distinct_leaf_hour`** macros for controller maintenance tags. **COOP/NYSE cache flush after policy controller events** — brief relearning surges mimic rapid moves; compare COOP **`statsEntity`** polls (optional side index) against endpoint counts; auto-suppress **`mobility_storm`** if flush markers appear in **`cisco:aci:syslog`**. **Anycast gateway ARP/GARP churn on SVI stretches** — VRF stretching between pods makes **`fvCtx`** **`vrfDn`** appear to hop even when East-West traffic reroutes logically; cross-check ServiceNow **`CHG`** approvals for L3Stretch operations and shorten alert windows rather than paging network security; document using **`aci_l3stretch_suppress`** lookups when **`vrfDn`** matches active change data.
